items.text: She is {1} honest person who studies at {2} university.

blanks.hint: follows the sound, not the letter

items.explanation: Article choice depends on the initial sound: 'honest' starts with a vowel sound, so 'an'; 'university' starts with a consonant /j/ sound, so 'a'.

items.text: I had {1} egg and {2} apple for breakfast.
items.explanation: Both nouns begin with a vowel sound, so each takes 'an'.
Unique things (the sun) and compass directions (the east) take 'the'.

items.text: I play {1} guitar, but I don't play {2} football.
items.explanation: Musical instruments take 'the' (play the guitar); sports and games take no article (play football).

items.text: She bought {1} new car. {2} car is bright red.
items.explanation: First mention of a countable noun takes 'a'; once it is known to the listener, the second mention takes 'the'.
